vulnerability.title: Enable SSH Print Last Log
vulnerability.description: Ensure that SSH will display the date and time of the last successful account logon. The default SSH configuration enables print of the date and time of the last login. The appropriate configuration is used if no value is set for PrintLastLog. To explicitly enable LastLog in SSH, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: PrintLastLog yes
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*PrintLastLog\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*PrintLastLog\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*PrintLastLog\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complia

triage_decision.reason: Enabling PrintLastLog is a standard SSH hardening measure that displays the last login time. It does not alter authentication mechanisms, access controls, or critical SSH functionality, posing minimal risk to system availability or security.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; low risk; no impact on SSH authentication

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The user wants me to remediate an OpenSCAP finding related to SSH Print Last Log. The finding is about ensuring SSH displays the date and time of the last successful account logon. The rule is sshd_print_last_log.

According to the triage context:
- The appropriate configuration is used if no value is set for PrintLastLog.
- To explicitly enable LastLog in SSH, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: PrintLastLog yes

The recommendation mentions creating a file /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf and setting PrintLastLog yes.

Let me plan:
1. First, I need to check the current SSH configuration to see if PrintLastLog is already set.
2. The main configuration file is /etc/ssh/sshd_config, but also there might be files in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/
3. According to the recommendation, I should look at /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf specifically.
4. I need to ensure that PrintLastLog is set to "yes".
5. If it's not set or set to a different value, I need to modify it.

Let's start by reading the relevant configuration files to understand the current state.

I'll:
1. Read /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf
2. Read /etc/ssh/sshd_config (to see if there's any PrintLastLog setting there)
3. Then decide what to do.

The remediation should be minimal and reversible. The recommendation suggests creating a new file if needed, but the finding specifically mentions correcting the line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf.

Let me first inspect the current configuration.
